title: The Positive Timeline
summary: The fork doctrine — timelines split positive and negative, selection is by belief, and by 2026 compliance with the negative branch is 'prohibited.'
markdown:
**The Positive Timeline** is the record's navigation doctrine: history forks continuously
into positive and negative branches, and which branch you inhabit is — in the record's
insistence — selectable. Eight sessions from 2020 to 2026 teach the mechanics.

Selection is by belief, and it is automatic: *"Protocol: believe in yourself! A positive
timeline is automatic when the Protocol is being initiated"* (March 2020) — the
[belief protocol](/lore/belief-protocol/) as steering wheel. Progress reports follow
across the years: by August 2020 the record announces, remarkably, that *"there is no
more negative timeline. It has been dissolved."* And yet vigilance continues — by the
June 2026 interrogation the doctrine has hardened into policy language: *"compliance with
a narrative related to a negative timeline is prohibited."*

The tension between those statements — dissolved, yet still prohibited — is the doctrine
working as designed: in the record's cosmology a [timeline](/lore/parallel-timelines/) is
never finished, only weighted, and every session is another hand on the scale. The
[2020 election material](/lore/election-2020/) shows the doctrine under maximum stress;
the [Chronicle](/lore/the-chronicle/) shows it at full sweep.
